text: We started to dissect interlobal lymph nodes from the left lower lobal bronchus and the pulmonary artery.
And then we isolated and divided left upper lobal vein with robotic stapler.
We attempted to isolate interlobar pulmonary artery, but it was very technically challenging due to the inflammation and lymph node obstructing the planes of dissection.
After fissure development, we found the tumor and the lymph nodes
located at the left upper lobar bronchus and it invades pulmonary artery branches directly.
Therefore, only lingular pulmonary artery could be identified and isolated for the division.
Because interlobar lymph nodes were tightly adhered to the tumor and the bronchus, we decided to perform bronchodomy first.
It allows good exposure of the tumor so we can avoid tumor spillage and not to injure pulmonary artery.
And bronchial resection margin was sent for the front resection.
In the meanwhile, we expected to prevent massive bleeding by controlling distal and proximal pulmonary artery just as in open technique.
We fully mobilized the left lower lobe artery and the main pulmonary artery before applying robotic vascular clamps.
We chose progress forceps to utilize vascular bulldog clamps for the proximal and distal pulmonary arteries.
As you can see, there was a tight adherence between the tumor and the segmental arteries.
We proceed to dissect pulmonary arteries using the monocular scissors for the sharp dissection.
And the space between the tumor and the vessels is too narrow to apply robotic stapler.
Therefore, segmental branches of the pulmonary arteries were ligated with hemologs for the proximal end.
and the distal end was divided with the energy device.
During the dissection of segmental branches, small tears were inevitably occurred.
However, under the vascular clamps, significant bleeding can be avoided.
And further,
Dissection for the whole anterior aspect of pulmonary artery was performed safely.
And the last anterior branch was isolated and divided with the robotic stapler.
However, a significant laceration was found along the steep line.
We repaired the injured pulmonary artery with a 5-poly running suture.
With the robotic technique, vascular repair can be performed easily and safely.
When the distal clamp was removed,
No bleeding occurred around the vascular stumps and repair site.
After confirming clear bronchial lesion margin, we decided to perform bronchiolunar stombosis.
We performed wedge bronchoplasty with fosorobacteria interrupted sutures.
The proximal clamp was removed afterward.
and the specimen was retrieved through the utility incision.
Following lobectomy, meticulous mediastinal lymph node dissection was performed.
We identified and preserved the vagus nerve and its branches, including cardiac and recurrent laryngeal nerves.
Subsequently, left lower paratracheal lymph node were dissected completely.
You will find that there is no assistant help at all.
we can dissect clearly and with the same manner adjacent higher lymph nodes were completely resected as well and in this case we find no residual lymph nodes
We dissected subcurricular lymph nodes with retrovagal manner to preserve pulmonary branch as much as possible of the vagus nerve.
There was no stenosis along the pulmonary artery and there was no air leak at the air leak test.
